focus: This 8 PM MDT run uses 2026-06-25 Garmin data through 19:41 MDT; sleep/HRV/readiness are available, while the day is still not quite final.
Primary recovery call: readiness 5/Poor, HRV Unbalanced, Body Battery 5, stress avg/max 47/98, and ACWR 1.5 High argue against more intensity tonight.
Activity context: Kane County Trail Running added 5.64 km, 741 kcal, avg/max HR 147/170, load 66.6, and helped push weekly intensity to 397/150 min.
Best signals: steps are 15,522/10,000, sleep stages improved vs yesterday with REM back online (16%), VO₂ max is 43.0, and weekly intensity goal is well exceeded.
Caution: sleep duration remains short (5h 03m vs 8h 50m need), HRV is below the balanced band, Body Battery is at 5, and stress is still classified stressful.
Nutrition log latest manual entry remains 2026-06-03: ~820 kcal, ~82g protein, water not logged; Garmin hydration shows 0 ml intake logged today despite ~945 ml estimated sweat loss.
Rollback to a sanitized public view is scheduled for July 3, 2026.
displayNotes: Evening Garmin data is more complete than the 5 AM run, but final sleep/body battery totals can still shift after midnight sync.
Use cards for scanability on iPhone: one primary number, status pill, and 2–3 details.
Use baseline/range visualization for HRV and RHR because personal baseline matters more than population comparison.
Use progress bars for readiness, sleep score, Body Battery, stress, steps, intensity minutes, training load, and nutrition.

Source fields include training readiness, HRV baseline, sleep score/duration/stages/need, Body Battery wake/current/high/charge/drain, stress average/max/rest split, steps/distance/active calories, weekly intensity minutes, acute load/ACWR, VO₂ max, nutrition calories/protein/water, resting HR vs 7-day average, SpO₂, and respiration.
